channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US and Iran Near Ceasefire Extension Amid Ongoing Negotiations
item.description: The United States and Iran have reportedly reached an agreement to extend the existing ceasefire and allow shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, while lifting certain US sanctions, though the deal is...

item.title: Canada conducts routine naval transit through Taiwan Strait amid Chinese opposition
item.description: A Canadian warship, HMCS Charlottetown, passed through the Taiwan Strait on May 22 and 23 as part of a routine operation, drawing a strong protest from China. The Chinese government condemned the tran...

item.title: Quezon City selected as host for Palarong Pambansa 2027 national school sports meet
item.description: Quezon City has been officially named as the host city for the Palarong Pambansa 2027, the Philippines’ premier national multi-sport event for student-athletes. The announcement confirms that the coun...

item.title: Jinggoy Estrada posts bail in graft case, remains free pending Sandiganbayan proceedings
item.description: Senator Jinggoy Estrada remains free after posting bail amounting to P90,000 in connection with a graft case linked to alleged anomalies in flood control projects. The bail was filed before the Sandig...

item.title: Why the ‘AI as a child’ metaphor hides labour, power, and accountability in AI systems
item.description: The article critiques the increasingly popular metaphor that ‘AI is like a child’, arguing that while it simplifies complex technology for public understanding, it also obscures critical realities abo...

item.title: SANDF Clarifies No Orders Issued to Confront June 30 Anti-Immigration Marchers
item.description: The South African National Defence Force (SANDF) has denied reports that it intends to deploy soldiers against citizens during the anti-immigration marches planned for 30 June. Earlier, the Justice, C...

item.title: How Genetics and Lifestyle Together Affect Health: A Tale of Two Friends
item.description: Vikram Anand and Sameer Chopra, both 45 and lifelong friends from Mumbai, discovered surprising insights about health after undergoing the Mira One integrated diagnostic test. Despite sharing the same...

item.title: Xbox Leadership Signals Strategic Changes as Microsoft Evaluates Future of Games, Studios, and Exclusivity
item.description: A newly circulated internal memo from Xbox CEO Asha Sharma has sparked widespread discussion about the future direction of Xbox under Microsoft. In the memo, Sharma emphasizes that the company is prep...

item.title: Kenyan president William Ruto invites student who mimicked him to State House as a nod to youthful talent
item.description: A Makueni High School student, Brian Mutua, gained viral attention after delivering a near-perfect impression of President William Ruto at a Limuru Girls High School talent showcase organized by Pace ...

item.title: Specialist divers lead rescue efforts in flooded Laos cave as several people remain trapped
item.description: Specialist cave divers are taking part in a high-risk rescue operation in Laos after multiple people became trapped inside a flooded cave system. According to reports, rescuers have already managed to...

item.title: Rising gambling participation in South Africa raises concerns over financial risk and debt
item.description: South Africa’s growing gambling industry is increasingly being linked to financial strain rather than entertainment, as many consumers turn to betting in response to economic pressure. The industry ge...

item.title: Molotov Cocktail Incident in North Vancouver Investigated as Possible Mistaken Identity
item.description: North Vancouver RCMP are investigating a frightening incident in which a Molotov cocktail was thrown through the window of a four-plex while a young family was inside. Fortunately, the father had just...

item.title: Arsenal face PSG in Champions League final as defensive strength takes centre stage
item.description: Arsenal are set for a high-stakes Champions League final clash against Paris Saint-Germain in Budapest, with both teams chasing European glory under managers Mikel Arteta and Luis Enrique. The London ...

item.title: UKZN student footballers signed to Durban City FC DDC squad for 2026/27 season
item.description: Three University of KwaZulu-Natal (UKZN) student footballers have been officially recruited into the Durban City Football Club (FC) DStv Diski Challenge (DDC) squad for the 2026/27 season. The announc...

item.title: Trump's Latest Medical Exam Confirms He Remains in Good Health
item.description: The White House announced that U.S. President Donald Trump is in excellent health following his most recent medical examination at Walter Reed National Military Medical Center on May 27, 2026. This wa...

item.title: Suspect injured after jumping from balcony while attempting to evade arrest in Batangas rape case
item.description: A man accused of rape was injured after reportedly jumping from a balcony in an attempt to escape arrest in Talisay, Batangas. According to reports, personnel from the Philippine National Police (PNP)...

item.title: Arsenal Supporters Undertake Complex Journeys to Attend Champions League Final in Budapest
item.description: Following Arsenal's recent Premier League triumph, fans are eagerly making their way to Budapest for the Champions League final against Paris Saint-Germain. With direct flights fully booked and prices...

item.title: NTSA Updates Traffic Fines System Allowing Motorists to Contest Minor Offences in Court
item.description: The National Transport and Safety Authority (NTSA) has rolled out an updated traffic fines system starting Monday, June 1, giving motorists more flexibility in handling minor traffic offences. Unlike ...

item.title: India and Russia review expansion of maritime, defence and strategic cooperation in Moscow talks
item.description: India’s National Security Advisor Ajit Doval held a series of high-level meetings in Moscow with senior Russian officials, including Nikolai Patrushev, Aide to the President and Chairman of Russia’s M...

item.title: Mothers Share Their Experiences Raising Children with Cleft Lip and Palate in South Africa
item.description: In South Africa, mothers are speaking out about the challenges and triumphs of raising children born with cleft lip and palate, a condition affecting the lip and roof of the mouth. Yvette Claassens fr...
